claimText: SACHET is an SMS-based disaster alert system operationalized by the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA).
evidenceExcerpt: SACHET is an SMS-based alert system operationalized by NDMA
examUse: Statement-based question on disaster management initiatives and their implementing agencies.
distractorHooks: Operationalized by the India Meteorological Department (IMD)
Operationalized by the Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services (INCOIS)

claimText: The SACHET alert system was developed by the Centre for Development of Telematics (C-DOT) utilizing the Common Alerting Protocol (CAP).
evidenceExcerpt: Developed by Centre of Development of Telematics C-DOT using the Common Alerting Protocol (CAP)
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the technological developer and protocol used for India's alert systems.
distractorHooks: Uses a proprietary closed-source protocol instead of the Common Alerting Protocol

claimText: Cell Broadcast alerts are faster than traditional SMS and are broadcast directly to all mobile phones within a specific affected area.
evidenceExcerpt: Cell Broadcast alerts are faster than SMS and are broadcast directly to all mobile phones in an affected area.
examUse: Comparison question evaluating the technological differences between Cell Broadcast and SMS for emergency alerts.
distractorHooks: Cell Broadcast requires users to download a specific application
Cell Broadcast is slower than SMS due to network congestion
Cell Broadcast requires users to manually subscribe to alerts

claimText: The Early Warnings for All (EW4All) initiative was launched at the UNFCCC COP27 in 2022 with the goal of ensuring universal protection by 2027.
evidenceExcerpt: Early Warnings for All (EW4All) was launched at COP27 in 2022 to ensure universal protection by 2027.
examUse: Statement-based question on international climate and disaster risk reduction targets.
distractorHooks: Aims to ensure universal protection by 2030
Launched at the Sendai Framework conference

claimText: The Early Warnings for All (EW4All) initiative is jointly led by the WMO, UNDRR, ITU, and IFRC.
evidenceExcerpt: EW4All is jointly led by WMO, UNDRR, ITU, and IFRC.
examUse: Institution-feature question identifying the leading organizations behind global disaster initiatives.
distractorHooks: Led exclusively by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
Led by the World Bank and IMF
Led solely by the UNFCCC secretariat
